spec_match.rationale: Accurately validates all primary schema structures of .well-known/related-website-set.json manifests (primary, associatedSites, serviceSites, ccTLDs) and asserts HTTPS scheme safety rules.

interactive.rationale: Contains a text editor box, click controllers to load valid/invalid configurations, and a Validate button that outputs parser errors, warnings, and mapping tables in real-time.

blurb_alignment.rationale: Aligned perfectly with the parent description: lets users paste manifests, validates them, shows active vs deprecated fields, and displays a migration guide.

edge_cases.rationale: Properly captures malformed JSON structures using robust try-catch blocks to prevent the browser validation logic from crashing.

references.rationale: The page contains absolutely no see-also, reference, or external link lists. It fails the repository-wide hard rule: 'Every feature and concept page must include a chromestatus.com/feature/<id> link'.

design_system.rationale: Conforms perfectly using standard fonts, custom buttons, borders, cards, and breadcrumbs.

openQuestions.title: Add mandatory ChromeStatus entry link and standard see-also footer
openQuestions.detail: To comply with the repository-wide hard rule ('Every feature and concept page must include a chromestatus.com/feature/<id> link'), we must add a 'see also' footer section containing a link to ChromeStatus entry 5194473869017088, alongside specifications referencing the RWS specification repository.

summary: A clean, highly educational utility that validates RWS JSON manifests. While it possesses rich interactivity, excellent error catches, and useful layout tables, it fails the repository's hard rule by omitting a direct link to the ChromeStatus database. Adding a standard see-also footer with this link is a high priority.
